title: astragalus hystrix
intro: Astragalus hystrix, also known as the Mongolian milkvetch or hairy astragalus, is a type of perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, prized for its adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that provide anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating effects, contributing to its medicinal properties. Astragalus hystrix is commonly prepared into teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is used to boost the immune system, alleviate fatigue, and enhance respiratory health.
